summary: Meta integrated improvement report cross-check follow-ups captured before planning closeout.

observations.problem: Checked-in batch manifest can keep sealed_clean_pass after canonical evidence artifacts are regenerated; --check detects digest drift, but the checked-in self-check/operator surfaces do not convert that drift into a first-class invalidation state.
observations.why_it_matters: Operators can see a sealed release claim beside newer canonical reports with different digests, weakening the release evidence authority ladder.
observations.automation_candidate: Promote artifact digest mismatch into a reusable closeout invalidation gate and include digest-watch rows in the self-check artifact.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-08: release-closeout-batch-manifest --check compares sealed artifact digests against live canonical artifacts, release-evidence-closeout-self-check records batch_artifact_digest_watch and fails on mismatch, and operator-release-summary exposes artifact_digest_policy_status plus mismatch/missing counts as a separate release axis.

observations.problem: The manifest generator can compute a current distribution ZIP in strict_review_release mode, but the checked-in manifest remains advisory with current_distribution_zip_known=false and active_reference_set_status=drift.
observations.why_it_matters: External review evidence cannot be tied to the exact distribution snapshot unless the review/closeout lane supplies the ZIP identity by default.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a review-release Make target or stricter closeout path that requires EXTERNAL_REPORT_CURRENT_DISTRIBUTION_ZIP_PATH and regenerates the manifest from the actual ZIP.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-08: external_report_reference_manifest accepts --basis-zip-path, strict Make closeout defaults the active basis to the current distribution ZIP, and the checked-in manifest now reports basis_current_match for build/release/LLMwiki-source.zip.

observations.problem: The reviewed snapshot includes tmp candidate JSON and pycache artifacts; the live workspace also contains many pycache files outside the two tools pyc paths named in the report.
observations.why_it_matters: Review snapshots become harder to distinguish from clean source packages, and ZIP inventory drift can obscure real release evidence changes.
observations.automation_candidate: Define a clean review snapshot/export profile that blocks tmp/*.candidate.json and tracked-surface pycache files before packaging or external review handoff.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-08: REVIEW_ARCHIVE_PROFILE now defaults to clean, release CI exports REVIEW_ARCHIVE_PROFILE=clean, review_archive.py enforces tmp/**/*.candidate.json plus public-surface __pycache__/*.pyc residue, and ops/reports/review-archive-report.json records machine-readable snapshot_hygiene status.

observations.problem: auto-improve readiness now emits learning_blocked_by_execution_not_runnable for status=not_runnable, but signoff revalidation only treats learning_blocked_by_review_required as the supported blocker and can classify not_runnable as not_required.
observations.why_it_matters: A queue-empty execution blocker should not be silently interpreted as no learning blocker when release and learning-claim gates are deciding whether human signoff is needed.
observations.automation_candidate: Promote learning blocker IDs into a shared vocabulary or helper and test not_runnable, review_required, and learning_likely transitions across readiness, generated report contracts, revalidation, and learning claim unlock review.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-08: not_runnable remains the canonical readiness state, learning_blocked_by_execution_not_runnable is treated as a signoff-unsupported active blocker, revalidation reports missing_signoff with a restore_runnable_proposal_queue decision option, and checked-in generated-report contracts now assert the not_runnable vocabulary.

observations.problem: Release smoke had an output-parent preflight, but a failing archive parent could stop before the partial report path was written when the report parent itself was still usable.
observations.why_it_matters: A live release ZIP rerun should fail in a bounded, machine-readable way so operators can distinguish unwritable archive output from archive construction, extraction, command timeout, or manifest drift.
observations.automation_candidate: Record output_parent_preflight in release-smoke reports and write a partial report for preflight failures whenever the report parent is writable.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-08: release_smoke now emits output_parent_preflight in final and partial reports, exits with a partial report on archive-parent preflight failure before calling the archive builder, and has regression coverage for that bounded failure path.
